label: Uncomplicated costochondritis without cardiopulmonary red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated costochondritis.
No cardiac/PE concern, unresolved dyspnea, hypoxia, hemoptysis, fever/systemic illness, trauma/fracture concern, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Cardiac or PE concern, pressure/exertional chest pain, shortness of breath, hypoxia, hemoptysis, syncope, fever, systemic illness, trauma/fracture concern, or diagnostic uncertainty.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed cardiology/pulmonary plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care if symptoms persist, recur, or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports costochondritis framing and supportive care. Autonomous Phase 1901-2000 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated costochondritis without cardiac/PE concern, unresolved dyspnea, hypoxia, hemoptysis, syncope, systemic illness, trauma/fracture concern, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports costochondritis framing and supportive care. Autonomous Phase 1901-2000 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated costochondritis without cardiac/PE concern, unresolved dyspnea, hypoxia, hemoptysis, syncope, systemic illness, trauma/fracture concern, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated costochondritis
no cardiac/PE concern, shortness of breath, hypoxia, hemoptysis, or trauma documented
